message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step approach:
1) Identify each main REG node and read the full GDPR text provided for that node.
2) Enumerate any explicit cross-references in the text of each main REG node and pull those referenced nodes from the provided referenced_nodes.
3) Compare the policy chunk against the combined requirements of each main REG node and its references.
4) Per the instructions, treat missing/omitted GDPR-required statements in the policy as Non-Deviation unless a contradiction or narrowing can be proven with direct, exact quotes from both GDPR and the policy.
5) Classify any proven differences with one taxonomy category and support with precise GDPR and policy quotes.

Findings by node:
- REG-025 (Art17(1)):
  • Considered references: REG-001 (Art6(1)), REG-009 (Art9(2)), REG-035 (Art21(1)), REG-036 (Art21(2)), REG-005 (Art8(1)).
  • The policy enumerates two erasure grounds: data no longer needed; consent withdrawn. It omits other GDPR grounds (unlawful processing, legal obligation to erase, offer of ISS to a child, objection grounds). Per rules, omissions are Non-Deviation.
  • Two provable deviations:
    - Severity deviation: GDPR requires withdrawal of consent AND no other legal ground; policy omits the latter qualifier, effectively broadening erasure.
    - Data deviation: GDPR requires erasure of personal data generally; policy specifies removal from “our IT systems and databases,” narrowing to certain media, despite also saying “stop processing.”
- REG-021 (Art15(1)):
  • Considered references: REG-041 (Art22(1)) due to Art15(1)(h).
  • The policy frames access as “whether and which personal data we store,” which narrows “are being processed” to “stored,” a Data deviation. Other Art15(1)(a)-(h) elements are absent, but per rules, omissions are Non-Deviation.
  • The policy’s limitation concerning “confidential information/professional secrecy” could interact with Art15(4)/Recital 63, but those provisions are not provided here; without a GDPR quote from the supplied nodes to evidence a mismatch, no deviation asserted on that point.
- REG-028 (Art18(1)):
  • Considered references: REG-035 (Art21(1)) due to Art18(1)(d).
  • The policy chunk does not address restriction of processing; per rules, omission = Non-Deviation. No provable contradiction.
- REG-017 (Art14(2)):
  • Considered references: REG-001 (Art6(1)), REG-009 (Art9(2)), REG-041 (Art22(1)).
  • The policy chunk does not provide Art14(2) transparency details; per rules, omission = Non-Deviation.
- REG-013 (Art13(2)):
  • Considered references: REG-001 (Art6(1)), REG-009 (Art9(2)), REG-041 (Art22(1)).
  • The policy chunk does not provide Art13(2) transparency details; per rules, omission = Non-Deviation.

Only assert deviations where the mismatch can be proven with exact quotes from the provided GDPR nodes and the policy text.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-025",
    "clause": "Art17(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-009", "REG-035", "REG-036", "REG-005"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "the data subject withdraws consent on which the processing is based according to point (a) of Article 6(1), or point (a) of Article 9(2), and where there is no other legal ground for the processing;",
        "policy_quote": "if the processing is based on your consent, you have withdrawn your consent.",
        "mismatch": "The policy grants erasure upon any withdrawal of consent without the GDPR’s qualifier that there must be no other legal ground for the processing.",
        "analysis": "By omitting the condition “and where there is no other legal ground,” the policy is more generous than GDPR, expanding the circumstances under which erasure is promised."
      },
      {
        "deviation_type": "Data deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "the controller shall have the obligation to erase personal data without undue delay",
        "policy_quote": "we must stop processing your personal data and remove it from our IT systems and databases.",
        "mismatch": "GDPR requires erasure of personal data generally, while the policy confines deletion to 'IT systems and databases,' narrowing the medium/scope.",
        "analysis": "Specifying only IT systems and databases potentially excludes other media or locations of personal data, narrowing the scope relative to the GDPR’s medium-neutral erasure obligation."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-021",
    "clause": "Art15(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Data deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "confirmation as to whether or not personal data concerning him or her are being processed",
        "policy_quote": "whether and which personal data we store about you.",
        "mismatch": "The policy narrows the Art 15(1) right from all processing to only what is stored.",
        "analysis": "Limiting the confirmation to stored data excludes processing operations beyond storage, narrowing the GDPR’s broader 'are being processed' scope."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-028",
    "clause": "Art18(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-035"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-017",
    "clause": "Art14(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-009", "REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-013",
    "clause": "Art13(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-009", "REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  }
]
```
